name: Circle the Shoresh
prompt_shape: Mark the shoresh letters in the token.
best_followup_correct: Strip affixes and explain what each non-root letter is doing.
best_followup_incorrect: Show prefix/root/suffix segmentation with one worked example.
software_notes: Direct digital replacement for the core R2 worksheet action.

name: Cross Out Non-Shoresh Letters
prompt_shape: Select the letters that are not part of the shoresh.
best_followup_correct: Ask what each removed letter contributes.
best_followup_incorrect: Contrast the same root with and without affixes.
software_notes: Strong digital fit; cleaner than cut-and-paste.

name: Split Word Into Parts
prompt_shape: Break the token into prefix, shoresh, and suffix parts.
best_followup_correct: Translate the whole token after naming each segment.
best_followup_incorrect: Pre-highlight one segment and ask the learner to finish the split.
software_notes: Best software replacement for the physical reassembly workflow in R2.

name: Classify Noun vs Verb
prompt_shape: Decide whether the token is a noun or a verb.
best_followup_correct: Route to the matching noun or verb morphology lane.
best_followup_incorrect: Show how the same ending means different things on nouns and verbs.
software_notes: A core routing archetype because later rules depend on this distinction.

name: Classify Past / Future / Present
prompt_shape: Choose whether the verb is past, future, or present/habitual.
best_followup_correct: Ask for person/gender/number or a short translation.
best_followup_incorrect: Show the specific prefix or suffix cue that drove the correct tense.
software_notes: Should be blocked for forms explicitly marked as ambiguous without context.

name: Identify Possessive Owner
prompt_shape: Identify who owns the noun from its ending.
best_followup_correct: Translate the noun phrase in English.
best_followup_incorrect: Contrast the same noun with two different possessive endings.
software_notes: Strong fit for reviewed exemplars because the family system is explicit in R3.

name: Identify Verb Subject
prompt_shape: Identify who is doing the action from the verb form.
best_followup_correct: Ask for a full gloss with the subject included.
best_followup_incorrect: Show the exact affix that encodes the subject slot.
software_notes: Best for verbs whose subject slot is not ambiguous.

name: Identify Attached Object on Verb
prompt_shape: Identify the object pronoun attached to the verb.
best_followup_correct: Ask who did what to whom in a short phrase.
best_followup_incorrect: Contrast subject endings and object suffixes on near-matching forms.
software_notes: Use only with clearly supported object-suffix families.

name: Translate Isolated Word
prompt_shape: Give the base meaning of the token.
best_followup_correct: Move immediately to phrase context or morphology explanation.
best_followup_incorrect: Provide a short glossary-supported clue rather than repeating the answer.
software_notes: Should be suppressed for low-information pronouns, particles, and context-heavy forms.

name: Translate Phrase in Pasuk
prompt_shape: Translate the highlighted Hebrew phrase in context.
best_followup_correct: Ask why the contextual English differs from the base gloss if it does.
best_followup_incorrect: Annotate the phrase by function word, root, and suffix before retrying.
software_notes: Best default replacement for weak standalone translation items.

name: Choose Correct Meaning From Options
prompt_shape: Choose the best meaning for the token or phrase from several options.
best_followup_correct: Ask which distractor was most tempting and why it was wrong.
best_followup_incorrect: Explain the rule or context cue that removes the wrong options.
software_notes: Distractors should reflect plausible confusion, not random vocabulary.

name: Color-Code Shared Shorashim
prompt_shape: Group or color tokens that share the same shoresh.
best_followup_correct: Ask how the affixes changed the role or meaning of each token.
best_followup_incorrect: Reduce the set to two obvious matches plus one distractor.
software_notes: Good anti-boredom format because it changes interaction shape without changing the skill.

name: Distinguish Command vs Past Plural
prompt_shape: Decide whether the form means they did or a direct command to many men.
best_followup_correct: Add a short phrase and ask the learner to justify the choice.
best_followup_incorrect: Show the same surface form once in each meaning and compare context.
software_notes: Good diagnostic lane because the ambiguity is explicitly taught in R3.
